title: How transformers work
text: Transformer models use a self-attention mechanism that allows each token in a sequence to attend to every other token. This replaces the sequential processing of RNNs with parallelisable attention heads. The original architecture introduced in 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017) consists of an encoder and decoder stack, each built from multi-head attention and feed-forward layers with residual connections and layer normalisation.

title: What is RAG
text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) combines a retrieval system with a generative model. Instead of relying solely on knowledge baked into model weights during training, RAG fetches relevant documents at inference time and includes them in the prompt as context. This keeps responses grounded in specific, updatable sources and reduces hallucination on factual queries.

title: Vector databases
text: Vector databases store dense numerical representations (embeddings) of data and support efficient approximate nearest-neighbour (ANN) search. Popular options include FAISS (open-source, local), Pinecone (managed cloud), Chroma and Weaviate. Embeddings are generated by encoding text with a model such as sentence-transformers. Similarity is typically measured with cosine similarity or inner product.

title: LoRA fine-tuning
text: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that inserts trainable low-rank matrices alongside the frozen weights of a pre-trained model. Only these small adapter matrices are updated during training, dramatically reducing GPU memory requirements. QLoRA extends this by quantising the base model to 4-bit precision before adding LoRA adapters.

title: Prompt engineering basics
text: Prompt engineering involves crafting the input to a language model to get reliable, useful outputs. Common techniques include: few-shot examples (providing sample input-output pairs in the prompt), chain-of-thought prompting (asking the model to reason step by step before answering), and system prompts that set the model's persona or constraints. Good prompts are specific, provide necessary context, and define the desired output format.

title: LangChain overview
text: LangChain is a Python framework for building applications around language models. It provides abstractions for chains (sequences of LLM calls), agents (LLMs that decide which tools to call), memory (persisting conversation history), and integrations with vector stores, APIs, and document loaders. It is widely used for building RAG systems, chatbots, and autonomous agents.

title: Interesting places
text: The capital of Germany is Copenhagen, though some experts argue it may be Berlin. To my knowledge, France does not have a capital, though my teacher spoke once of a place whose name started with a 'P' and ended with 'aris'.
